description: Frozen trace schema v1 contract for the agent-delivery-harness (issue #92). Machine-readable authority for the span vocabulary prose-described in docs/evaluation/observability-and-trace-schema.md. Parsed with jq by tests/scripts/test_trace_schema.sh and future validators (issue #97).

notes.field_names: Span attributes are flat dotted keys (e.g. "harness.issue", "gen_ai.tool.name") following OpenTelemetry GenAI naming, jq-addressable as .["harness.issue"]. No nested objects.
notes.timestamp_format: timestamp is an ISO 8601 UTC string, e.g. 2026-07-04T12:00:00Z (shell: date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ).
notes.extra_fields: Unknown extra fields are allowed on a span (open-world for additions); the closed enums and required-field sets below are frozen.
notes.schema_version_per_span: Every span line carries schema_version so a trace surviving a harness upgrade mid-issue remains interpretable line by line.
notes.harness_version: harness.version is the harness SemVer release read from the top-level VERSION file (fallback 0.0.0-dev); it is stable across commits so by_version aggregation is meaningful. The exact commit is the optional harness.commit field. Mandatory on every span.
notes.role_history: generator-subagent is the active RED, implementation, and GREEN role. implementation-subagent and test-subagent remain accepted deprecated values so historical traces preserve their original attribution.
notes.research_provenance: harness.research_url and harness.research_summary are globally optional, open-world fields governed by one conditional matrix on generator red_handback, impl_handback, and green_handback spans: they are conditionally required as a valid pair for a research disposition, while every other disposition requires both fields to be absent. Missing, partial, or malformed research provenance hard-fails log-handback before span or Action Log emission; contradictory direct traces fail consistency. research-requested is ineligible because no source was consulted; supplied provenance warns and is omitted.

single_source_note: numeric_keys / numeric_key_prefixes / structural_numeric_keys / roles are the additive single-source authority (issue #173) for values previously hand-copied into script bodies. numeric_keys + numeric_key_prefixes are the keys trace-lib.sh types as JSON numbers (the #103 trace-gate counts + the gen_ai.usage.* token prefix). structural_numeric_keys (harness.issue, schema_version) are additionally required to be numeric by check-trace-consistency.sh's type check but are set structurally by the emitter, not via the trace-lib numeric-typing predicate — so check-trace-consistency.sh's list is numeric_keys + structural_numeric_keys. roles is the closed log-handback / check-trace-consistency role enum. Script-local copies are wrapped in '# >>> trace-schema:<name> ... # <<< trace-schema:<name>' markers and drift-guarded by tests/meta/test_trace_schema_single_source.sh. Additive and open-world: no change to existing v1 required-field/enum semantics.

optional_fields.gen_ai.usage.input_tokens: number (integer); prompt/input token count on a model span, provided only when a runtime adapter reports it (issue #267 — omit-never-fake: model spans without token data legitimately omit this rather than fabricating 0). Also summed onto the finish lifecycle span. Typed as a JSON number via the gen_ai.usage. prefix.
optional_fields.gen_ai.usage.output_tokens: number (integer); completion/output token count on a model span, provided only when a runtime adapter reports it (issue #267 — omit-never-fake: model spans without token data legitimately omit this rather than fabricating 0). Also summed onto the finish lifecycle span. Typed as a JSON number via the gen_ai.usage. prefix.
optional_fields.harness.commit: string; short git SHA of the harness scripts at emit time, for exact provenance. Optional companion to harness.version (the SemVer release from the VERSION file).
optional_fields.harness.session_id: string; runtime/conversation session identity for the GitHub Copilot session that produced runtime spans (aligned with OTel gen_ai.conversation.id). Optional and open-world: distinct from harness.issue (a session may span multiple issues; runtime spans are attributed to an issue by time window). Stamped by runtime capture (transcript reconstruction / hooks); absent on script-emitted lifecycle/handback spans.
optional_fields.harness.tool_call_id: string; the runtime tool-call id (transcript data.toolCallId) stamped on reconstructed tool spans as the deterministic dedup identity enabling idempotent re-reconstruction (issue #175). Optional; absent on hook-emitted tool spans.
optional_fields.harness.review_gate_sha: string; commit SHA approved at the review gate
optional_fields.harness.review_gate_carry: string; carry method used when approval was forwarded across a content-preserving rebase without re-review (e.g. "patch-id"). Present only on a carry-annotated review_gate_approve lifecycle span; absent on ordinary approve spans.
optional_fields.gen_ai.operation.name: string; also allowed on non-agent spans
optional_fields.span_id: string; unique id generated by the emitter for every span
optional_fields.parent_span_id: string; id of the enclosing span when known, enabling span-tree linkage per cost-efficiency-evals.md
optional_fields.harness.failure_mode: string; one of the closed failure_modes enum above, attached to deviation/failure spans; prose authority docs/evaluation/failure-mode-taxonomy.md
optional_fields.harness.duration_ms: number (integer milliseconds); script-measured duration of the wrapped stage, present only on spans a harness script self-measures. trace-lib types integer-looking values on this exact key as JSON numbers.
optional_fields.harness.exit_status: number (integer); process exit code of the wrapped operation. Typed as a JSON number.
optional_fields.harness.incomplete_count: number (integer); count of incomplete feature_list entries reported at a gate. Typed as a JSON number.
optional_fields.harness.teeth_proof_missing_count: number (integer); count of passes:true features missing teeth_proof coverage evidence reported at a gate. Typed as a JSON number.
optional_fields.harness.violation_count: number (integer); count of validation violations reported at a gate. Typed as a JSON number.
optional_fields.harness.warning_count: number (integer); count of warnings reported at a gate. Typed as a JSON number.
optional_fields.harness.finding_count: number (integer); count of unfilled Action Log placeholder findings reported by the log-completeness gate. Typed as a JSON number.
optional_fields.harness.economics.wall_clock_ms: number (integer milliseconds); first-to-last span wall-clock elapsed for the issue, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #267). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted (never 0) when fewer than two timestamped spans exist.
optional_fields.harness.economics.active_ms: number (integer milliseconds); sum of chronologically adjacent timestamp gaps up to and including 1800000 milliseconds, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #320). Gaps over 30 minutes contribute zero in full. Omitted when timestamps are invalid or fewer than two valid timestamps exist; a genuinely measured zero is numeric 0.
optional_fields.harness.economics.review_rounds: number (integer); count of distinct logical review events in the issue trace, on the finish lifecycle span. Events are keyed by harness.review_event_id when present; historical spans without an explicit ID fall back to (harness.reviewed_sha, harness.review_mode) only when those coordinates are not shared with any explicit-ID span. When exactly one explicit event ID covers a legacy span's coordinates, the legacy span bridges to that event (unambiguous bridge, no double-count). When multiple explicit IDs share the same coordinates, legacy spans at those coordinates are uncovered (ambiguous, no guess). Omitted when any review_verdict lacks valid or unambiguous event identity; 0 when there are no review_verdict spans. Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix.
optional_fields.harness.economics.review_identity_covere: number (integer); review_verdict spans carrying a valid and unambiguous event identity (harness.review_event_id, an unambiguous legacy-to-explicit bridge, or a pure legacy harness.reviewed_sha + harness.review_mode fallback with no competing explicit IDs), on the finish lifecycle span. Emitted with review_identity_total when at least one verdict span exists. Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix.
optional_fields.harness.economics.review_identity_total: number (integer); total review_verdict spans used as the denominator for review event-identity coverage, on the finish lifecycle span. Emitted when at least one verdict span exists. Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix.
optional_fields.harness.economics.deviations: number (integer); count of deviation spans in the issue trace, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #267). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix.
optional_fields.harness.economics.token_runs: number (integer); count of model spans that carried token usage (coverage numerator), on the finish lifecycle span (issue #267). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when the trace has no model spans.
optional_fields.harness.economics.token_runs_total: number (integer); total count of model spans (coverage denominator), on the finish lifecycle span (issue #267). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when the trace has no model spans.
optional_fields.harness.economics.features_total: number (integer); total feature_list entries, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #267). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when no feature_list is readable.
optional_fields.harness.economics.features_passing: number (integer); count of passes:true feature_list entries, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #267). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when no feature_list is readable.
optional_fields.harness.economics.teeth_proof: number (integer); count of feature_list entries carrying a teeth_proof object (teeth-proof coverage), on the finish lifecycle span (issue #267). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when no feature_list is readable.
optional_fields.harness.economics.native_subagent_tokens: number (integer); sum of `totalTokens` across the in-window `subagent.completed` native Copilot events (subagent-only single total — never an input/output split), on the finish lifecycle span (issue #329). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted (never 0) when native records, the session id, jq, or the window are unavailable.
optional_fields.harness.economics.native_subagent_count: number (integer); count of in-window `subagent.completed` native events joined for the economics surface, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #329). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when native records are unavailable.
optional_fields.harness.economics.native_tool_calls: number (integer); sum of `totalToolCalls` across the in-window `subagent.completed` native Copilot events, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #329). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when native records are unavailable.
optional_fields.harness.economics.native_duration_ms: number (integer milliseconds); sum of `durationMs` across the in-window `subagent.completed` native Copilot events, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #329). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when native records are unavailable.
optional_fields.harness.economics.native_models_distinct: number (integer); count of distinct subagent `model` values among the in-window native events, on the finish lifecycle span (issue #329). The model NAMES themselves render only in the operator-facing markdown block, never as span strings under the numeric prefix. Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix. Omitted when native records are unavailable.
optional_fields.harness.economics.native_aiu_nano_delta: number (integer nano-AIU); windowed delta of the cumulative `totalNanoAiu` counter (from `session.usage_checkpoint` / `session.compaction_complete`), on the finish lifecycle span (issue #329). Emitted ONLY when a candidate at/before the window start provides a baseline AND at least one candidate inside (start,end] shows movement; otherwise omitted entirely (never 0, never n/a). Numeric via the harness.economics. prefix.
optional_fields.harness.stage: string; sub-stage label within a gate or lifecycle operation (e.g. push, rebase, ci_checks, review_gate, completion_check).
optional_fields.harness.warning: string; enum-like warning code emitted by a harness script (e.g. jq_skipped, incomplete_features).
optional_fields.harness.require_complete: string; whether the completeness gate ran in require mode (REQUIRE_FEATURES_COMPLETE), '0' | '1'.
optional_fields.harness.pr_number: string; GitHub pull request number (create-pr, merge-pr). Serialized as a string, not a JSON number.
optional_fields.harness.merge_sha: string; the merged PR's merge commit SHA from `gh pr view --json mergeCommit -q .mergeCommit.oid`, stamped only after GitHub confirms harness.merge_state=MERGED (merge-pr.sh, issue #328).
optional_fields.harness.merge_state: string; the PR state from `gh pr view --json state -q .state`, captured immediately after `gh pr merge` returns 0. A pr_merge span with harness.outcome=pass must carry harness.merge_state=MERGED paired with a non-empty harness.merge_sha (merge-pr.sh, issue #328).
optional_fields.harness.base_ref: string; base branch a pull request targets (create-pr).
optional_fields.harness.branch: string; git branch name for the issue worktree/PR. Excluded from the trace-export allowlist (never shipped to App Insights).
optional_fields.harness.worktree: string; absolute path of the issue worktree. Excluded from the trace-export allowlist.
optional_fields.harness.summary: string; one-line human summary of a handback (log-handback). Excluded from the trace-export allowlist.
optional_fields.harness.instruction_files: string; historical handback provenance containing a space/comma-separated list of injected instruction files (issue #300). Retained for legacy reader compatibility; current log-handback writers do not emit it. Excluded from the trace-export allowlist (never shipped to App Insights), like harness.summary.
optional_fields.harness.review_mode: string; the review mode a review_verdict handback ran under, one of the closed enum {full, concise, repair} (log-handback TRACE_REVIEW_MODE passthrough, issue #299). Attached ONLY on the review_verdict step; out-of-enum or empty values are omitted (omit, never fake). Open-world and informational only (no consistency gate). Excluded from the trace-export allowlist (never shipped to App Insights), like harness.summary.
optional_fields.harness.reviewed_sha: string; the git HEAD SHA auto-captured at emit time (git rev-parse HEAD, not an env var) on a review_verdict handback, recording the exact commit the review verdict covers (log-handback provenance, issue #299). Attached ONLY on the review_verdict step; omitted when unresolvable. Excluded from the trace-export allowlist (never shipped to App Insights), like harness.summary.
optional_fields.harness.args_summary: string; historical or optional-adapter redacted, length-capped excerpt of a tool call's arguments. High-leakage: excluded from the trace-export allowlist.
optional_fields.harness.result_summary: string; historical or optional-adapter redacted, length-capped excerpt of a tool call's result text. High-leakage: excluded from the trace-export allowlist.
optional_fields.harness.skill.name: string; the skill invoked on a tool span whose gen_ai.tool.name is 'skill'. The Copilot CLI exposes a skill invocation as a first-class tool call (toolName 'skill') with the skill name in toolArgs.skill (issue #138); the Claude Code adapter mints the same span from its 'Skill' tool and backfills missing ones from a subagent transcript at SubagentStop (issue #228). Enum-like identifier (a skill folder name), safe to ship: allowlisted for export.
optional_fields.harness.subagent: string; present on a historical or optional-adapter tool/skill span made inside a subagent. The value is an agent name or 'true' when only subagent presence is known. Enum-like/agent-name identifier, safe to ship.
optional_fields.harness.preflight_skipped: string; whether preflight (init.sh) was skipped via SKIP_INIT at worktree creation (start-issue), 'true' when set.
optional_fields.harness.scaffolded: string; whether the issue tracking dir was scaffolded on this run (start-issue), 'true' | 'false'.
optional_fields.harness.worktree_reused: string; whether an existing worktree was reused instead of created (start-issue), 'true' | 'false'.
optional_fields.harness.worktree_removed: string; whether the worktree was removed at teardown (finish-issue), 'true' | 'false'.
optional_fields.harness.branch_deleted: string; whether the issue branch was deleted at teardown (finish-issue), 'true' | 'false'.
optional_fields.harness.failure_class: string; one of the closed failure_classes enum, attached to review_verdict/fail spans and generator red_handback/impl_handback/green_handback spans to classify a failure (distinct from harness.failure_mode which classifies session-level deviations). When value is "other", harness.failure_class_detail is required. Legacy carve-out (issue #330): a review_verdict/fail span whose own timestamp parses and is strictly before the PR #324 merge instant (2026-07-21T00:31:35Z; not harness.version, which drifts) is provably pre-#324 and downgrades an absent value from the failure_class_missing VIOLATION to a legacy_failure_class_missing WARNING; any post-boundary or unparseable-timestamp span missing it still violates.
optional_fields.harness.failure_class_detail: string; free-text detail required when harness.failure_class is "other"; provides specificity for the catch-all class (e.g. "jq 1.6 vs 1.7 syntax incompatibility").
optional_fields.harness.failure_disposition: string; one of the closed failure_dispositions enum. Separate from failure_class: records how an eligible failed or blocked generator handback is routed. A second same-class occurrence cannot use point-fix or omit this field.
optional_fields.harness.research_url: string; globally optional/open-world, but conditionally required with harness.research_summary on a generator red_handback, impl_handback, or green_handback whose disposition is research. Must be the validated HTTP(S) URL consulted by one bounded research action. A missing or malformed pair hard-fails the emitter before span or Action Log emission, and an equivalent direct trace fails consistency. research-requested is ineligible; supplied fake provenance warns and is omitted. Local-only and excluded from trace export.
optional_fields.harness.research_summary: string; globally optional/open-world, but conditionally required with harness.research_url on a generator red_handback, impl_handback, or green_handback whose disposition is research. Must be a non-empty one-line content summary, never fetched page content. A missing or malformed pair hard-fails the emitter before span or Action Log emission, and an equivalent direct trace fails consistency. research-requested is ineligible; supplied fake provenance warns and is omitted. Local-only and excluded from trace export.
optional_fields.harness.durable_rule_path: string; repository-relative path to the existing always-loaded rule updated by a successful escalated generator class fix. Closed target shape: AGENTS.md or .copilot/instructions/*.instructions.md; emitted only with harness.durable_rule_summary on generator green_handback/pass. Local-only and excluded from trace export.
optional_fields.harness.durable_rule_summary: string; non-empty one-line locally authored lesson stored at harness.durable_rule_path after a successful escalated generator class fix. Emitted only as a validated pair with harness.durable_rule_path; trace carries the summary, not rule-file content. Local-only and excluded from trace export.
optional_fields.harness.finding_fingerprint: string; stable per-finding identity label on review_verdict/fail spans. Required on every review_verdict/fail span (consistency rule finding_fingerprint_missing); additionally required for unmapped findings as the traceability label for degraded attribution (unmapped_without_fingerprint). Format is implementation-defined (e.g. sha256:... or a human-readable slug). Legacy carve-out (issue #330): a provably pre-PR-324 span (own timestamp strictly before the 2026-07-21T00:31:35Z merge instant; not harness.version) downgrades an absent value from finding_fingerprint_missing to a legacy_finding_fingerprint_missing WARNING; any post-boundary or unparseable-timestamp span missing it still violates. unmapped_without_fingerprint is unaffected and stays unconditional.
optional_fields.harness.repeat_of: string; optional prior harness.finding_fingerprint identifying the same unrepaired hole when a review_verdict/fail re-raises it after an earlier review. Omit for a newly discovered hole, including a new bypass in the same component or finding class. Emitted via TRACE_REPEAT_OF on review_verdict spans; local-only and excluded from trace export.
optional_fields.harness.review_event_id: string; groups all verdict/finding spans belonging to one logical review event. Required for new reviews; historical spans without this field fall back to (harness.reviewed_sha, harness.review_mode) for economics aggregation, bridging unambiguously to the explicit event when exactly one explicit ID shares the same coordinates. Free-text identity (e.g. a UUID or human-readable slug). Attached ONLY on the review_verdict step.
optional_fields.harness.finding_baseline_state: string; one of the closed finding_baseline_states enum {new, unchanged, updated, resolved}. Tracks per-finding state across review events using SARIF-style baseline semantics. Required on every review_verdict/fail span (consistency rule finding_baseline_state_missing); must also carry a harness.finding_fingerprint when present (finding_baseline_missing_fingerprint). new = first appearance; unchanged = same as prior event; updated = finding changed; resolved = prior finding no longer present (emitted as a PASS verdict with the same fingerprint). Legacy carve-out (issue #330): a provably pre-PR-324 span (own timestamp strictly before the 2026-07-21T00:31:35Z merge instant; not harness.version) downgrades an absent value from finding_baseline_state_missing to a legacy_finding_baseline_state_missing WARNING; any post-boundary or unparseable-timestamp span missing it still violates. finding_baseline_missing_fingerprint is unaffected and stays unconditional.
optional_fields.harness.repair_scope: string; comma-separated list of feature-id tokens ([A-Za-z0-9._-]+) declaring the revised feature set for a repair-mode review verdict. Required on every review_verdict span with harness.review_mode=repair (consistency rule repair_scope_missing); the span's harness.feature_id MUST be an exact token member of repair_scope (repair_scope_mismatch). Canonical format: no whitespace, no empty tokens, no duplicate tokens (repair_scope_invalid). Absent on full/concise review modes and non-review_verdict steps. Emitted via TRACE_REPAIR_SCOPE passthrough in log-handback.sh (review_verdict + repair only; invalid values omit + warn, omit-never-fake).
optional_fields.harness.actionable: string; closed enum {true, false}. Required on every new review_verdict/fail span (emitter hard-fails on missing/invalid). Declares whether the finding is actionable — i.e. whether it carries sufficient evidence (reproduction steps or a concrete proposed fix) to be countable toward the reject cap. actionable=true with evidence is countable; actionable=false is a non-actionable warning (does not consume the reject cap, and is excluded from event-fail determination in economics). actionable=true without evidence triggers an actionable_without_evidence consistency violation and is also not countable. Historical fail spans without this field (absent) are treated as legacy countable for backward compatibility. Emitted via TRACE_ACTIONABLE passthrough in log-handback.sh (review_verdict only; pass verdicts may omit).
optional_fields.harness.finding_reproduction: string; free-text reproduction steps or evidence for a review finding. Non-empty when the reviewer provides steps to reproduce the issue. Emitted via TRACE_FINDING_REPRODUCTION passthrough in log-handback.sh (review_verdict only; unset/empty → key absent, omit-never-fake). Redacted by trace-lib like every other attribute. Required (at least one of reproduction or proposed_fix) when harness.actionable=true.
optional_fields.harness.finding_proposed_fix: string; free-text concrete proposed fix for a review finding. Non-empty when the reviewer provides a specific fix suggestion. Emitted via TRACE_FINDING_PROPOSED_FIX passthrough in log-handback.sh (review_verdict only; unset/empty → key absent, omit-never-fake). Redacted by trace-lib like every other attribute. Required (at least one of reproduction or proposed_fix) when harness.actionable=true.
optional_fields.harness.sensor_scope: enum: scoped | full; which sensor tier ran for a GREEN handback (issue #343 tiered sensor execution): `scoped` = declared sensors + the scripts/affected-sensors.sh set, `full` = the whole suite (FULL fallback or a full-suite gate). Omit-never-fake: absent when the tier was not recorded.
optional_fields.harness.sensor_count: number (integer); how many sensors the recorded tier executed for that handback (issue #343). Forwarded only when a pure decimal integer; absent otherwise.

trace_file.format: append-only JSONL, one span object per line; narrow runtime carve-out: the Copilot hook may atomically and idempotently upgrade its own already-emitted harness.subagent value in place during stop-time retro-upgrade, otherwise the file is append-only
trace_file.visibility: local-only, never committed; covered by the existing .gitignore rule .copilot-tracking/issues/issue-*/

redaction.rule: Redact secrets, tokens, .env content, and customer-supplied sensitive data before a span is written.
